meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing Strategies to Scale Your First App
content.seo_description: Stop building free apps. Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to monetize your projects using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert beginner checklist.
content.answer_block: Moving beyond free apps requires integrating robust payment infrastructure like Stripe. By using Antigravity for rapid scaffold generation and Claude Code to implement secure webhooks, you transform a student project into a professional SaaS. Pricing strategy isn't just math; it is the bridge between a hobby and a career.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in the terminal, debugging local environments and pushing commits to GitHub. Your app works, but it feels like a ghost town—a collection of features with no validation. The 'tutorial hell' trap is real: you build for yourself, not for users, and every feature feels like a chore because there's no signal of market demand.

Real-world software is defined by the exchange of value. When you add pricing, you shift from being a 'coder' to an 'AI Builder.' This checklist is your blueprint to move past the basic CRUD apps, integrate professional payment rails, and finally understand what makes a SaaS product actually viable in the wild.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify what your user pays for. Is it per-seat, per-API call, or a flat monthly subscription?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS feature set and suggest three pricing tiers based on a per-user, per-feature, and per-usage metric. Provide a table comparing these models.

steps.title: Scaffold Stripe Integration
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate for Stripe Checkout sessions, ensuring secure payment handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure backend route for creating a Stripe Checkout session. Include logic for handling Success and Cancel URLs.

steps.title: Implement Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Your app needs to 'listen' to Stripe when a payment succeeds. This is critical for unlocking premium features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure webhook listener for Stripe events including 'checkout.session.completed' and 'customer.subscription.deleted'.

steps.title: Database Schema for Subscriptions
steps.description: You need to store user plan statuses. Update your schema to track subscription IDs and current billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the User model to include 'stripe_customer_id', 'subscription_status', and 'plan_type'.

steps.title: Deploy and Test in Sandbox
steps.description: Verify your flow using Stripe's CLI to simulate real payments before going live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that mocks a successful Stripe webhook payload to verify my backend billing logic.

pro_tips: Always offer a 'Yearly' discount toggle; it dramatically increases your upfront cash flow and reduces churn.
Never build custom billing logic; use Stripe's Customer Portal to let users manage their own subscriptions.
Implement a 'usage-based' limit early, even if it's just a soft cap, to prepare your app for scale.
Use environment variables for all Stripe keys; never hardcode your API secrets, even in development.
Send a welcome email automatically via your billing webhook; it increases retention by 30%.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on building an analytics dashboard to track your MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) and user conversion rates.
content.conversion_block: You have the technical roadmap to turn your code into a business. Don't let your project sit in a private repo—get it in front of users and start collecting feedback. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
